description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a rugged, angular form and a partially hollowed-out section visible from one side. Its surface is predominantly gray with patches of brown and rusty orange, suggesting mineral deposits or weathering. The stone features a rough, matte texture with visible grain and natural fractures, giving it an authentic, unpolished appearance. One side displays a cavity or indentation, while another side has red alphanumeric markings, possibly for cataloging or identification. The stone's edges are uneven and jagged, with some smoother worn areas indicating exposure to the elements. Overall, it appears dense and solid, suitable as a natural obstacle or barrier in an urban or landscaped setting.
description: A rugged, gray stone with brown and orange weathering, featuring a rough texture, jagged edges, and a partially hollowed side with red markings.
description_view_0: This view shows the broadest, flattest face of the stone, highlighting its mottled gray and brown coloration and rough surface.
description_view_1: This angle reveals the hollowed-out cavity on one side, emphasizing the stone's irregular geometry and depth.
description_view_2: This perspective displays the top and one side, including the red alphanumeric markings and more pronounced surface fractures.
description_view_3: This view focuses on the stone's jagged, angular edges and the transition between gray and brown areas, with less visible cavity.
attribute_location: urban park, landscaped median, or roadside verge
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or used as a natural barrier along paths or edges
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling and veining with patches of brown and orange mineralization; red painted markings on one side.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered with visible surface pitting, minor erosion, and some smoother worn areas; no significant lichen or moss.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts moderately with manicured or paved urban environments but can blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements; may accumulate debris or vegetation in crevices.

license_info.creator_display_name: UQ School of Earth and Environmental Sciences
